_about: Parity check: same submission archive scored by upstream `programbench eval` and by the BenchFlow onramp verifier. Each row pairs the upstream-recorded eval.json (.ref/programbench/src/programbench/data/test_runs/) with what tests/test.sh produces inside the BenchFlow image.
_pipeline: Generate task -> docker build -> stage submission to /app/ -> bash /tests/test.sh -> read /logs/verifier/{reward.txt,score.json}
_methodology_match: Both pipelines: (1) wipe /workspace, stage submission, run compile.sh -> ./executable; (2) snapshot the build, restore for each branch; (3) score = passed / (junit_cases + missing_from_junit), counting tests at full {classname}.{name}.

subset._note: Fixture instance shipped with ProgramBench. Validates the verifier path end-to-end against the upstream-recorded oracle eval for both a correct and an incorrect submission. No live model is needed because the upstream-shipped submission archives are deterministic.

real_instance_smoke._note: End-to-end exercise of the verifier against a real ProgramBench cleanroom image, using a stub `compile.sh` that emits a no-op executable. Demonstrates: (a) the BenchFlow Dockerfile builds against the real :task_cleanroom image; (b) the verifier resolves all 14 active test branches; (c) HuggingFace blob fetch + per-branch tarball extract + pytest runs all succeed; (d) reward computation matches the expected 0/N for a non-functional submission.

upstream._note: Upstream `programbench eval` against the same stub also reports score=0 — but as a top-level error (XmlParseError on the first branch's results.xml) rather than per-branch zeros. Both pipelines agree the submission is non-functional; they disagree on the granularity of the failure record. Full agreement is expected for any submission that produces parsable JUnit XML.

_blockers: 200 cleanroom Docker images, each multi-GB, gated behind Docker Hub pulls — out of scope for this sandbox.
8.2 GB of test blobs from HuggingFace dataset programbench/ProgramBench-Tests fetched on-demand by the verifier.
Per-task wall time on Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite is hours (programs span 30k–80k LoC); full set needs Daytona/Modal.
full_set._how_to_run: uv run python -m benchflow.job onramp/programbench/run_programbench.yaml --override environment=daytona; then for each instance pull submission.tar.gz from the trial dir and feed it through `programbench eval` for upstream score, comparing in onramp/programbench/parity.py.
